Six Days · Rajasthan, India · December 2026

Jaipur
& Bundi

Two cities, one road through open Rajasthan countryside between them — and the feeling, by the end, that you've seen something the guidebooks haven't quite caught up with yet.

01

Days 1–2

Jaipur

Sat 5 – Sun 6 December

02

Days 3–6

Bundi

Mon 7 – Thu 10 December

The Vibe

Jaipur announces itself — pink sandstone walls, forts on every hillside, a city that has been performing its own grandeur for three centuries and knows exactly how good it is at it. Bundi, three and a half hours south, does the opposite. Blue-washed houses climb a hillside with no particular interest in being noticed. A crumbling palace rises above them. A 14th-century fort crowns the whole thing. Stepwells appear around corners with no signage. Monkeys occupy the rooftops without apology. Together, they make the case for Rajasthan better than anywhere else.

The Itinerary

Six Days in Rajasthan

Jaipur · Sat 5 – Sun 6 Dec

Day One · Sat 5 Dec — Arrival

The Pink City

Arrive into Jaipur throughout the day — it's the collection point, and the old city absorbs you immediately. Hawa Mahal turns amber as the sun drops behind it. The Johari Bazaar is all jewellery and noise and colour. First stop on the food trail: Rawat Misthan Bhandar for pyaz kachori — a deep-fried pastry of spiced onion that has defined this city's street food for generations — then Lassiwala on MI Road for the thick makhaniya lassi served from the same stall for 80 years. Group dinner together at Bar Palladio: candlelit courtyard, Jaipur's creative crowd, excellent cocktails. The trip properly begins.

Day Two · Sun 6 Dec — Jaipur

Forts, Mirrors and the City Below

Amber Fort early — a UNESCO World Heritage Site built from 1592 across two centuries. Go before the heat. On the walk back, Panna Meena Ka Kund: a 16th-century stepwell of perfectly symmetrical crisscrossing staircases, almost always quiet despite being 10 minutes from Amber. Afternoon: City Palace and Jantar Mantar as a pair, 400 metres apart. The City Palace is still a functioning royal residence; Jantar Mantar is an 18th-century astronomical observatory with instruments of extraordinary scale — the world's largest sundial among them. A guide is essential here or it reads as abstract sculpture. Nahargarh Fort for sunset: the entire city lit up below the ramparts, the best photograph of the trip.

Bundi · Mon 7 – Thu 10 Dec

Day Three · Mon 7 Dec — The Drive

Into the Countryside

Morning departure after breakfast. The 3.5-hour drive through open Rajasthan countryside: scrubland gives way to rock formations, small villages appear and disappear, the landscape grows quieter and stranger as the road descends. Then the blue city resolves itself from the hillside — the palace above it, the fort higher still. The haveli receives you. Claim a room, find the rooftop, order chai. Afternoon: first wander through the old city lanes and bazaar. Sundowners as the fort lights up. Dinner in town.

Day Four · Tue 8 Dec — The Palace

Garh Palace and Taragarh Fort

The Badal Mahal — the Cloud Palace — is the reason to come to Bundi. A room of floor-to-ceiling murals painted between 1687 and 1707, vivid blue-greens and ochres depicting Krishna's court, hunting scenes, celestial armies. They have been here for three centuries and they look it, and that is entirely the point. A guide is not optional. Chitrashala gallery, Hall of Public Audience, the marble coronation throne. Then the climb to Taragarh Fort above — largely unrestored, largely empty, the entire blue city spread below you in its improbability. Evening: bazaar walk and street chai.

Day Five · Wed 9 Dec

Stepwells, the River & the Cenotaph

Morning stepwell walking tour with a local guide — Raniji ki Baori descends 150 feet through tiers of carved sandstone built in 1699. Bundi has over 50 of them through its lanes, most unmarked and unvisited. A guide finds them. Afternoon: Chambal River safari — narrow gorges, gharial crocodiles motionless on sandbars, marsh birds in extraordinary numbers. Back by late afternoon to the 84-Pillared Cenotaph at golden hour: built in 1683 in memory of the Maharaja's nurse, 84 carved white sandstone pillars, lit beautifully in the early evening. Nawal Sagar Lake on the way back — the half-submerged temple of Varuna in still water, the whole city reflected around it. Final group dinner.

Day Six · Thu 10 Dec — Travel Day

Sukh Mahal, Then Chennai

One last stop: Sukh Mahal, the small lakeside pleasure palace where Kipling stayed and found the material for Kim. Thirty minutes, leaves an impression disproportionate to its size. Then the road back to Jaipur (3.5 hours), arriving early afternoon with time to spare. Flight departs Jaipur 17:25, arrives Chennai 20:00. Check in, settle in — the wedding starts tomorrow.

Where You'll Stay in Bundi

The Haveli Braj Bhushanjee has been in the same family for 200 years — the same family that produced four of Bundi's Prime Ministers in the 19th century. It sits on the road to the palace, the fort visible from the rooftop at every hour and illuminated at night in a way that makes it difficult to go to bed. Several rooms carry original Bundi School murals on the walls. The Darikhana dining room, where the family has fed travellers for generations, serves food that is simple, vegetarian, and quietly exceptional.

Also Worth Knowing

Getting There

Plan to arrive at Jaipur Airport on Saturday 5 December — flights connect from most major Asian cities. The group spends two nights in Jaipur, then drives to Bundi on Day 3 — 3.5 hours through open Rajasthan countryside.

Jaipur Accommodation

Book your own hotel in Jaipur for two nights — aim for the old city or Bani Park area to be close to everything. Heritage havelis are the right call: Samode Haveli and Narain Niwas Palace are the standouts if availability allows.

The Flight to Chennai

Jaipur → Chennai departs 17:25 on Thu 10 Dec, arrives 20:00. Drive back to Jaipur airport by early afternoon — comfortable timing after a morning at Sukh Mahal.

Why December in Rajasthan

Rajasthan in December runs at 25°C by day and cool enough for a layer by night. The post-monsoon green hasn't faded, the afternoon light is long and golden, and the crowds that clog Jaipur and Udaipur thin considerably by the time you reach Bundi. It is, without much competition, the best month to be here.

Food Highlights

Dal baati churma — wheat dumplings fired in wood coals, cracked open with spiced lentils and a sweet crumble — is the dish that defines this part of Rajasthan. Rawat Misthan Bhandar for pyaz kachori. Lassiwala on MI Road for the makhaniya lassi. The Darikhana at the haveli in Bundi. Street chai everywhere, in clay cups, always.

The Full Trip at a Glance

The Side Trip

Sat 5 Dec

Arrive Jaipur. Group assembles. Street food and Bar Palladio.

Sun 6 Dec

Amber Fort, Panna Meena Ka Kund, City Palace, Jantar Mantar, Nahargarh at sunset.

Mon 7 Dec

Drive to Bundi. Check into the haveli. First evening in the blue city.

Tue 8 – Wed 9 Dec

Garh Palace, Taragarh Fort, stepwells, Chambal safari, 84-Pillared Cenotaph.

Thu 10 Dec

Sukh Mahal. Drive to Jaipur. Fly 17:25 → Chennai 20:00.

The Wedding

Thu 10 Dec — Evening

Arrive Chennai. Check in, settle in.

Fri 11 – Sun 13 Dec

Wedding festivities across three days in Chennai.

Mon 14 Dec

Depart Chennai. Fly home.

What to Book

  • ✦ Flight into Jaipur by 5 Dec
  • ✦ Two nights accommodation in Jaipur
  • ✦ Flight out of Chennai on 14 Dec
  • ✦ Let us know if you're joining the Bundi trip
  • ✦ Everything else is taken care of

Everyone goes to Jaipur. The people who've been to Bundi don't tell you about it — they just go back.
